title: Postpartum anemia: foods and follow-up
excerpt: Causes after birth, symptoms, iron-rich meals, supplements, and emergency signs — follow your clinician’s labs.
content:
## Causes

Blood loss at delivery, prior anemia, or low intake while breastfeeding.

## Signs

Fatigue, dizziness, pale skin, breathlessness — confirm with blood tests.

## Foods

Lean red meat, liver in moderation (cooked), eggs, beans, dark greens + vitamin C (orange, guava). Continue prescribed iron tablets; take as directed for absorption.

## Supplements

Do not double-dose iron without labs; constipation is common — fiber and fluids help.

## Emergency

Chest pain, fainting, or heart palpitations — seek urgent care.

metaDescription: Iron-rich foods and recovery after postpartum anemia — medical follow-up essential.
